summary: Retest of two fixes from iteration_2.json — both PASS, plus all regression checks PASS. Teen self sign-up (age 14) now correctly redirects from / to /kid/{childId} via Root()->TeenHome (App.js lines 30-46) which GETs /children and navigates to first child; home-signout is visible, parent Profiles UI (goto-parent-dashboard) is not shown. PIN race is fixed: Profiles.js initializes hasPin from user.has_pin in AuthContext on first render (line 21: useState(!!user?.has_pin)), so an immediate click on goto-parent-dashboard after login opens the PIN modal every time and blocks /parent navigation; wrong PIN (0000) shows pin-error, correct 2468 navigates to /parent. Regression: demo parent login persists on refresh; under-13 (age 10) teen signup blocked with friendly auth-error; fresh parent signup sees pin-tip and goes directly to /parent.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: Profiles.js still calls api.get('/auth/me') in useEffect to refresh hasPin (line 37) even though AuthContext already has it. Harmless now (initial state from context prevents race) but redundant network call; could be removed.

TeenHome in App.js calls /children unconditionally on every mount; consider memoizing or using user.child_id (returned by /auth/register) to avoid the extra request and the loading flicker on refresh.
server.py CORS still allows '*' fallback with allow_credentials=True — works for Bearer tokens but should be tightened to explicit origins for prod.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5: teen redirect, PIN race, refresh persist, under-13 blocked, fresh-parent no-PIN)

seed_data_creation: Created throwaway @example.com users during the playwright run (TeenTess teen account with auto-child, one under-13 user not persisted due to backend rejection, one FreshP parent). All harmless; left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both reported fixes verified. App.js Root() now routes role==='teen' to <TeenHome/> which navigates to /kid/{first child id}. Profiles.js hasPin seeded from user.has_pin via AuthContext, removing the race that previously let users bypass the PIN modal. No new bugs identified.
rca of the issue: N/A — both issues already fixed by main agent; this iteration was verification only.
